name: Ibew 141
description_short: IBEW Local Union 141 is a trade union for electricians in the Ohio Valley, offering member services including training, continuing education, and union representation—not a consumer finance company.
description_long:
IBEW Local Union 141 is a labor union chartered in 1902 that represents electricians and electrical workers in the Wheeling, West Virginia area and the broader Ohio Valley region. The union has operated continuously for over 120 years, with documented history dating back to 1893. It is affiliated with the International Brotherhood of Electrical Workers and serves as a professional organization for skilled trades workers in the electrical industry.

The union's primary offerings include apprenticeship programs, continuing education courses, member representation, and union benefits. Members must complete a nationally certified 5-year training program with a minimum of 8,000 hours of on-the-job training. The union provides ongoing professional development through courses in CPR, electrical code (NFPA 70e), confined space safety, first aid, OSHA 10 certification, motor controls, rigging, foreman training, and equipment-specific instruction. Members receive support in obtaining and maintaining their West Virginia Electrical license and must pass drug testing and OSHA safety training as requirements of membership.

What distinguishes IBEW Local 141 is its emphasis on comprehensive, nationally certified electrical training standards and continuous professional development requirements. The union prioritizes workplace safety with mandatory drug testing and safety certifications. Members benefit from on-the-job representation with local contractors and union support for both apprentices and experienced electricians. The organization also hosts member community events such as retiree dinners and golf scrambles.

This is a legitimate labor union providing trade training and representation services, not a consumer finance provider. It was miscategorized as a "payday-alternative" lender. The organization does not offer loans, credit services, or emergency cash advances of any kind. Members access union benefits through dues payments and participation in the apprenticeship and continuing education programs.
services: 8,000+ hours of on-the-job training with local contractors
MSHA and confined space safety training
Grounding and bonding per NEC instruction
West Virginia Electrical license support and maintenance
pros: Nationally certified 5-year apprenticeship program with minimum 8,000 hours hands-on training
Comprehensive continuing education including CPR, OSHA, confined space, NFPA 70e, and specialized electrical training
Mandatory drug testing and OSHA safety training requirements ensure high safety standards
Members obtain and maintain West Virginia Electrical licenses through the union
On-the-job representation and support from the union with local electrical contractors
Long operational history since 1902 with continuous service to the electrician community
Member benefits including community events and access to secure member portal
cons: Not a consumer finance provider—offers no loans, credit products, or emergency cash services
Membership requires successful completion of demanding 5-year apprenticeship; not accessible to those seeking immediate employment
Limited to electricians and electrical workers in the Ohio Valley region; geographic and professional restrictions
Website provides minimal detail on dues structure, benefits packages, or specific member services beyond training
best_for: Individuals seeking apprenticeship training in electrical work with comprehensive national certification
Licensed electricians in West Virginia seeking professional development, continuing education, and union representation
Electrical contractors and employers in the Ohio Valley seeking trained, union-certified workers
diagnosis: IBEW Local Union 141 is a trade union for electricians, not a consumer finance company. It provides apprenticeship training, continuing education, and union representation—not loans or credit services. The categorization as a "payday-alternative" lender is fundamentally incorrect and should be reassigned to 'banking' or removed entirely, as it is a professional labor organization with no lending, credit, or emergency cash products.
typical_results_timeline: Membership approval and account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions are usually faster than traditional banks.
